name: Ivan Lins
abstract: Ivan Guimarães Lins (born 16 June 1945) is a Latin Grammy-winning Brazilian musician. He has been an active performer and songwriter of Brazilian popular music (MPB) and jazz for over fifty years.

concerts.label: Salute: Antonio Carlos Jobim

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve recorded four duets with him. Actually the fourth duet is the song. He sent me the song to write the lyrics to and then we recorded this duet for this album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: He called him up. “George Benson loves your music. Yeah, we’re thinking about doing two of your songs.” ... The next day, that cat was up here from Brazil, and he was directing them ... We went in the studio. We worked for about – I don’t know – maybe a month, every day for a month, all night in the
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: Ivan Lins, the first time I heard him was some songs that he had written that he sent to Sergio, he’s like check this guy out. Beautiful songs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I did a song with the Brazilian. Ivan Lins. ... I Wish I’d Met You. I love that. That’s from the Turned to Blue.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: like Ivan Lins you know. That’s another thing that like I think about, man when you — like bossa nova harmonies
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: Ivan Lins is a great favorite of mine. And we’ll do some concerts later in October.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
